title: Brain Slice Preparation for Electrophysiology Recording
author: Sarah van Veen

steps.title: Prepare 500 mL 1 x Choline...
steps.instruction: Prepare 500 mL 1 x Choline Chloride solution (slicing and recovery solution). %s %s

steps.title: Prepare 250 ml 1 x Recording...
steps.instruction: Prepare 250 ml 1 x Recording solution (dilute and add NaHCO 3 and D-Glucose) %s %s

steps.title: Prepare 250 ml 1 x Holding...
steps.instruction: Prepare 250 ml 1 x Holding solution (dilute and add NaHCO 3 and D-Glucose) %s %s

steps.title: On day of cutting, prepare holding...
steps.instruction: On day of cutting, prepare holding and recordings acsf from 10x stock.

steps.title: Place 250 mL of the choline...
steps.instruction: Place 250 mL of the choline chloride cutting solution in -20°C or -80°C freezer for 30-40 min or 10-15 min, respectively. Medium should be partly frozen when used.

steps.title: Keep precooled cutting solution on ice...
steps.instruction: Keep precooled cutting solution on ice and bubble with carbogen (at least 00:15:00 before use).

steps.title: Place beaker with other 250 mL...
steps.instruction: Place beaker with other 250 mL choline chloride in 32 °C waterbath and bubble with carbogen.

steps.title: Place beaker with 250 mL holding...
steps.instruction: Place beaker with 250 mL holding solution at Room temperature and bubble with carbogen.

steps.title: Insert cutting blade in vibratome, if...
steps.instruction: Insert cutting blade in vibratome, if feasible/available use blade alignment (e.g. vibrocheck).

steps.title: Check availability/preparation before starting dissection
steps.instruction: Check availability/preparation before starting dissection

steps.title: Add precooled cutting solution to dissection...
steps.instruction: Add precooled cutting solution to dissection buffer tray (which is cooled using chiller or ice) and keep bubbling with carbogen.

steps.title: Set parameters (speed (0
steps.instruction: Set parameters (speed (0.08-0.10 mm/s), amplitude (1.15-1.30 mm), and section thickness (250 µm) on the vibratome control panel.

steps.title: Add 1-2 ml isoflurane onto tissue...
steps.instruction: Add 1-2 ml isoflurane onto tissue on bottom of sedation box and cover with lid.

steps.title: Gently peel off the skull using...
steps.instruction: Gently peel off the skull using blunt forceps, starting on top of the cerebellum. Try to push/peel the skull away from the head.

steps.title: Make to small incisions perpendicular to...
steps.instruction: Make to small incisions perpendicular to the midline above the olfactory bulb.

steps.title: Make an incision down the midline...
steps.instruction: Make an incision down the midline using sharp surgical scissors up to the front of the brain (up to olfactory bulb).

steps.title: Cut the skin down the midline to expose the skull
steps.instruction: Cut the skin down the midline to expose the skull.

steps.title: Place the decapitated head on cuttingboard
steps.instruction: Place the decapitated head on cuttingboard. Removal of brain must be done as quickly as possible, ideally within 30-45 seconds.

steps.title: Take the animal from the sedation...
steps.instruction: Take the animal from the sedation box and immediately decapitate with large scissors.

steps.title: After 2-3 minutes place animal in...
steps.instruction: After 2-3 minutes place animal in sedation box and wait until animal is stationary and is breathing slowly (after ~20-30 seconds)

steps.title: Place the brain on the superglue...
steps.instruction: Place the brain on the superglue area with the rostral part up.

steps.title: Make sure the specimen disc is...
steps.instruction: Make sure the specimen disc is dry and put a drop and smear out a drop of superglue.

steps.title: Remove access cutting solution and remove cerebellum
steps.instruction: Remove access cutting solution and remove cerebellum.

steps.title: Take the brain from the cutting...
steps.instruction: Take the brain from the cutting solution and place on a cutting board.

steps.title: Dry a precooled specimen plate
steps.instruction: Dry a precooled specimen plate.

steps.title: Let the brain cool for 2-3...
steps.instruction: Let the brain cool for 2-3 minutes in the cutting solution.

steps.title: Gently flip out the brain of...
steps.instruction: Gently flip out the brain of the skull using a small spoon and drop it directly into the cold cutting solution in the vibratome.

steps.title: Once brain is fully exposed, make...
steps.instruction: Once brain is fully exposed, make an incision along the coronal plane in between the olfactory bulb and the rostral part of the cortex.

steps.title: Add some cold cutting solution to...
steps.instruction: Add some cold cutting solution to the brain to cool and solidify the glue.

steps.title: Place specimen disc in buffer tray...
steps.instruction: Place specimen disc in buffer tray and position the cortex towards the blade.

steps.title: After all slices have been collected...
steps.instruction: After all slices have been collected and recovered, turn off water bath and leave brain slices in beaker with holding solution for 1 hour to fully recover.

steps.title: Keep each slice in recovery solution...
steps.instruction: Keep each slice in recovery solution for 6 minutes and then store them in the holding solution at room temperature. Make sure not to mix too much choline chloride solution into the holding solution when transferring the slices.

steps.title: Position razor blade at desired location/height...
steps.instruction: Position razor blade at desired location/height and perform first cut by pressing the “RUN/STOP” button.

steps.title: After slice has been collected immediately...
steps.instruction: After slice has been collected immediately place in recovery solution at 32°C.

steps.title: Start cutting sections of 250 um of the required area
steps.instruction: Start cutting sections of 250 um of the required area.

steps.title: Repeat desired region of the brain (e
steps.instruction: Repeat desired region of the brain (e.g. primary visual cortex) has been reached.

steps.title: Reposition blade (500-800 um) and perform second cut
steps.instruction: Reposition blade (500-800 um) and perform second cut.

steps.title: Return the blade to the initial...
steps.instruction: Return the blade to the initial position using the blade backwards button on control panel.

steps.title: After blade has successfully cut through...
steps.instruction: After blade has successfully cut through the entire brain, remove rostral cut part.

steps.title: Wash acsf through the setup for...
steps.instruction: Wash acsf through the setup for 5 minutes and discard this volume.

steps.title: For these experiments we added TTX...
steps.instruction: For these experiments we added TTX (1 uM) and AP-5 (100 uM) to external recording solution to specifically focus on mEPSCs (recorded at -70 mV) and mIPSCS (recorded at 0 mV) in L2/3 of primary visual cortex. However, the above mentioned slice preparation method can be used for many different types of electrophysiological experimental designs tailored to the requirements of the ongoing project.

steps.title: Internal medium used to record mEPSCs...
steps.instruction: Internal medium used to record mEPSCs and mIPSCs in primary visual cortex: %s %s

steps.title: For recordings we used borosilicate glass recording pipettes (resistance 3
steps.instruction: For recordings we used borosilicate glass recording pipettes (resistance 3.5–5 MΩ, Sutter P-1000).

steps.title: Recordings are done at 32-33°C
steps.instruction: Recordings are done at 32-33°C

steps.title: Electrophysiology solutions are prepared as either...
steps.instruction: Electrophysiology solutions are prepared as either 1x (choline chloride) or 10x (recording & holding acsf) stock solutions and (if required) diluted for use on the day of the experiment. NaHCO 3 & D-glucose are added fresh during dilutions to working solutions. We use ~1 week of shelve life for all solutions at 4°C.
